title: Investigate Tables
description: Work with Windchill tables to organize and display product data. Personalize table layouts, create custom views, and explore the Windchill Next Gen UI.

topics.title: Windchill Table Structure

content.text: Throughout Windchill, product data is presented in tables. Objects appear as rows and their attributes appear as columns. Every time you open a folder, view search results, or look at your Home page, you are working with tables. Understanding how tables work - and how to manipulate them - is essential for working efficiently with large datasets.

Tables support two broad categories of interaction. First, you can view information - reading the data displayed in rows and columns, sorting it, filtering it, and adjusting the layout to focus on what matters. Second, you can perform actions on information - selecting objects and using toolbar icons or the Actions menu to check out, revise, delete, or otherwise modify those objects.

rows: Toolbar and Actions Menu
Perform actions on selected objects such as check out, revise, or delete
Use toolbar icons above the table or right-click an object to open the Actions menu
Pick a View
Switch between predetermined views, apply filters, or customize the table layout
Click the dropdown arrow next to the current view name above the table
Search in Table
Filter the table to show only objects containing a specified text string
Type a search term into the Search in table field above the table

content.text: Use Search in table to quickly filter large datasets. Type any text string and the table instantly narrows to show only objects whose visible column values contain that string. Click the red X to clear the filter and restore the full table.

Beyond these three key tools, you can manipulate columns directly. Adjust column width by dragging the column border, reorder columns by dragging column headers left or right, and sort rows by clicking a column header. Some tables also have a Table Display Options button for additional features like "View selected object." Drag handles at the bottom of a table let you resize the table height. The number, content, and order of rows and columns strongly affects your ability to work efficiently with large datasets.

content.prompt: Match each table feature to its function:

pairs.right: Perform actions like check out, revise, or delete on selected objects
Switch between predetermined views or customize the table layout
Filter the table to show only objects containing a specified text string
Reorder columns by dragging them to a new position in the table

keyTakeaways: Windchill presents product data in tables with objects as rows and attributes as columns
Tables support two categories of interaction: viewing information and performing actions on information
The three key table tools are the Toolbar/Actions menu, Pick a View, and Search in table
Columns can be resized, reordered, and sorted directly by interacting with column headers

topics.title: Table Personalization and Custom Views

content.text: Windchill tables are highly personalizable. You can move columns, lock them in place, group data by column values, and create entirely custom views that persist for future use. These capabilities let you tailor any table to match your specific workflow and the information you need most.

cards.back: Drag a column header left or right to reposition it in the table. For example, drag the Name column to the left of Number to put the most useful information where you need it.
Hover over a column header, click the dropdown arrow, and select Lock. A locked column stays visible when you scroll horizontally through wide tables. Unlock it using the same dropdown menu.

Hover over a column header, click the dropdown, and select Group By This Field. The table sorts and groups objects based on that column's values (e.g., group by Version). Toggle off by clearing the Show in Groups checkbox.
Hover over the locked column header, click the dropdown arrow, and select Unlock. You must unlock a column before you can use Group By - grouping is not available while any column is locked.

content.text: You cannot use Group By while a column is locked. If you need to group by a field, unlock all locked columns first, then apply the grouping.

Custom views let you save a specific combination of object filters, column selections, and sort orders for reuse. Administrators commonly create custom views for their teams, but any user can create personal custom views for their own efficiency. Once created, custom views appear in the Pick a View dropdown and are available every time you return to that table.

content.prompt: Arrange the steps to create a custom view in the correct order:
items: Open the Pick a View dropdown and select Customize
Click the New View icon and name the view
Select the object types to include (e.g., Part)
Add filter criteria (e.g., State = In Work)
Set the sort order and click Finish

keyTakeaways: Columns can be moved by dragging, locked to stay visible during horizontal scrolling, and grouped by field value
You cannot use Group By while a column is locked - unlock columns first before grouping
Custom views save a combination of object filters, column selections, and sort orders for reuse
Both administrators and individual users can create custom views for personal efficiency

topics.title: Windchill Next Gen UI

content.text: Starting with Windchill 13.1.2, PTC introduced the Windchill Next Gen UI - a collection of modernized features that enhance the user experience. Next Gen focuses on modernizing the Home page and enhancing folder browsing, offering a more streamlined and customizable interface alongside the classic Windchill experience.
Switching Between Classic and Next Gen

The classic Home page remains the default in Windchill. To switch to the modernized experience, click the "New Home" toggle in the upper-right corner of the Home page. Similarly, click the "New Folders" toggle to activate the enhanced folder browsing experience. You can switch back to the classic UI at any time using the same toggles.
Classic UI vs. Next Gen UI

rows: Home Page Layout
Traditional table-based layout with customizable table order
Modernized and simplified layout with quick actions and dynamic auto-updating graphs
Finding Recent Data
Browse through Recent Products in the Navigator
Easily find recently used data directly from the Home page
Folder Browsing
Standard table view with basic column controls
Powerful table controls with smart column filtering, multiple-level grouping, and auto-resizing columns
Favorites & Export
Basic favorites functionality
New favorites and export capabilities for folder contents
Column Management
Manual column resizing and reordering
Auto-resizing columns with the ability to pin columns in place

content.text: The Next Gen UI aims to minimize the need for custom applications by providing a customizable, modern interface out of the box. As new functionality is added with each Windchill release, the Next Gen UI continues to close the gap between what organizations need and what Windchill provides natively.

content.prompt: Match each Next Gen UI feature to the benefit it provides:

pairs.right: Faster access to frequently performed tasks without navigating through menus
Real-time visual summaries of product data status without manual refresh
More precise control over which data is visible in folder tables
Organize folder contents by several attributes simultaneously for deeper analysis

keyTakeaways: Windchill Next Gen UI is a collection of modernized features introduced starting with Windchill 13.1.2
Switch to Next Gen using the "New Home" and "New Folders" toggles in the upper-right corner
Next Gen provides quick actions, dynamic graphs, smart filtering, and multiple-level grouping
Next Gen aims to minimize the need for custom applications by offering a customizable native interface

topics.title: Personalize Tables

content.text: In this exercise, you will navigate to a product folder and personalize the table by reordering columns, locking a column in place, and grouping data by a column value.
This exercise builds on your familiarity with the Belt Sander product context. Make sure you are signed in to Windchill as Casey Johnson before proceeding.

content.title: Personalize Tables
content.objective: Navigate to a product folder and personalize the table by reordering columns, locking columns, and grouping data.

steps.action: Click the Browse link in the Navigator and expand the Belt Sander product.
steps.detail: Access the product context to view its data.

steps.action: Click Folders in the Browse pane and select the Windchill Parts folder.
steps.detail: This folder contains the parts for the belt sander product.

steps.action: Click the red X next to the Search in table field to clear any existing filter.
steps.detail: Start with the full unfiltered table view.

steps.action: Drag the Name column header from the right side to the left side of the Number column.
steps.detail: Reordering columns puts the most useful information where you need it.

steps.action: Hover over the Name column header and click the dropdown arrow that appears.
steps.detail: The column dropdown provides table manipulation options.

steps.action: Click Lock to lock the Name column in place.
steps.detail: Locked columns remain visible when you scroll horizontally through wide tables.

steps.action: Scroll right to verify the Name column stays visible, then scroll back.
steps.detail: This confirms the lock is working correctly.

steps.action: Hover over the Name column header dropdown and click Unlock.
steps.detail: You need to unlock the column before you can perform Group By operations.
steps.hint: Cannot Group By while a column is locked.

steps.action: Hover over the Version column header and click the dropdown arrow.
steps.detail: Grouping helps organize related objects together.

steps.action: Click "Group By This Field" to sort the table based on object versions.
steps.detail: Objects are now organized by their version number.

steps.action: Review how the table is now grouped by version.
steps.detail: This helps identify which objects are at the same version level.

steps.action: Hover over the Version column header dropdown again.
steps.detail: You can toggle grouping off to return to the normal view.

steps.action: Clear the "Show in Groups" checkbox to remove the grouping.
steps.detail: Returns the table to its standard flat view.

keyTakeaways: Columns can be reordered by dragging their headers to a new position
Locking a column keeps it visible when scrolling horizontally through wide tables
Group By organizes table rows by a column's values, but requires all columns to be unlocked first
The Show in Groups checkbox toggles grouping on and off without losing your column layout

topics.title: Create a Custom Table View

content.text: In this exercise, you will create a custom table view that displays only In Work parts with specific columns and a sort order, then apply that view for immediate use.

content.title: Create a Custom Table View
content.objective: Create a custom table view to display only In Work parts with specific columns and sorting.

steps.action: In the Navigator Browse pane, navigate to Belt Sander > Folders > Windchill Parts.
steps.detail: Start from the folder containing the parts you want to view.

steps.action: Click the dropdown arrow to the right of the Folder Contents view name.
steps.detail: This opens the Pick a View list.

steps.action: Select Customize from the Pick a View list.
steps.detail: Customize opens the view configuration dialog.
steps.hint: You may need to scroll down in the Pick a View list.

steps.action: Click the New View icon and type "All Working Windchill Parts" in the Name field.
steps.detail: Choose a descriptive name that reflects the filter criteria.

steps.action: In the Description field, type "Windchill parts currently in the In Work state" and click Next.
steps.detail: A description helps others understand what the view shows.

steps.action: Clear the "Select all rows" checkbox and select only the Part checkbox, then click Next.
steps.detail: This filters the view to show only part objects.

steps.action: Select State in the Criteria field, click Add, set the Value to "In Work", and click Next.
steps.detail: Adding state criteria limits results to parts currently being worked on.

steps.action: Hold Ctrl and select Last Modified, Modified by, State, and Version, then click the Add arrow to move them to Selected Columns.
steps.detail: These columns provide the most useful information for tracking work in progress.

steps.action: Click Next, set the First Sort column to Name, and click Finish.
steps.detail: Sorting by name makes it easy to locate specific parts alphabetically.

steps.action: Click OK to confirm the refresh, then select "All Working Windchill Parts" from the Pick a View list.
steps.detail: Your custom view is now available for reuse whenever you return to this table.

keyTakeaways: Custom views are created through the Pick a View > Customize > New View workflow
Each custom view specifies object types, filter criteria, visible columns, and sort order
Custom views appear in the Pick a View dropdown for quick reuse
Creating personal custom views improves efficiency when working with large or complex tables
